informal: Prove that the intersection of any collection of subspaces of $V$ is a subspace of $V$.
lean:
theorem Axler_exercise_1_8 {F V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Field F]
  [Module F V] {ι : Type*} (u : ι → Submodule F V) :
  ∃ U : Submodule F V, (⋂ (i : ι), (u i).carrier) = ↑U
reasoning: The informal claim says: the intersection of any collection of subspaces of V is itself a subspace. The Lean signature fixes a field F, a vector space V (AddCommGroup + Module F V), and an arbitrary index type ι with a family u : ι → Submodule F V, then asserts the existence of a Submodule U whose carrier equals ⋂ i, (u i).carrier. Saying "there exists a submodule whose underlying set equals this intersection" is exactly the assertion that the intersection set is a subspace. The index type being an arbitrary Type* faithfully captures "any collection," and the empty-ι case (where the intersection is all of V) is correctly handled since V itself is a submodule. No extra hypotheses (e.g. finiteness, nonemptiness) are imposed, and no part of the conclusion is weakened.
